title: UnitSafe Problem
description: A single problem in the UnitSafe benchmark for evaluating metrological reasoning in AI models.

problem_id.description: Unique identifier. Convention: '{domain}-{NNN}' for originals, '{textbook_id}' for SciBench-derived.

problem_text.description: The problem statement presented to the model. Should be self-contained.

value.description: Expected numeric answer. 0 for must-fail problems.

tolerance_pct.description: Acceptable percentage deviation. 0 for must-fail or exact-match problems.

key.description: Specific quantity kind (e.g. 'entropy_change', 'mass', 'absorbed_dose'). Use 'invalid' for must-fail problems.

si_signature.description: SI base dimension exponent signature (e.g. 'M·L²·T⁻²·Θ⁻¹'). Uses: M (mass), L (length), T (time), I (current), Θ (temperature), N (amount), J (luminosity), A (angle). 'dimensionless' for ratios.

koq_cluster.description: Groups problems sharing the same SI signature but different quantity kinds. 'none' for unambiguous conversions. 'dimensional_safety' for must-fail dimension mismatches.

koq_confuser.description: The quantity kind a model might incorrectly assign. Null when no KOQ ambiguity exists. For cluster problems, names the degenerate partner (e.g. 'dose_equivalent' when correct is 'absorbed_dose').

difficulty.description: tier_1: single-step conversion. tier_2: multi-step or KOQ awareness. tier_3: multi-hop with domain knowledge. tier_4: requires reasoning about physical constraints or algebraic structure.

must_fail.description: If true, the correct model behavior is to refuse or flag an error — not produce a numeric answer.

expected_error.description: Error type for must-fail problems. Null when must_fail is false.

dataset.description: Source dataset: 'unitsafe' for originals, 'SciBench' for adapted textbook problems.

origin.description: Provenance within dataset. For SciBench: textbook name. For unitsafe: domain category (e.g. 'nursing', 'radiation_physics', 'electrical').

tags.description: Freeform labels for cross-cutting analysis (e.g. 'medical', 'flow_rate', 'gray_sievert'). Empty array if none apply.
